title: Material scope
action: Run first. Auto-confirm company is in scope. If any exemption is claimed (household, law enforcement), quarantine chunk and halt pipeline — route to human before proceeding.

title: Territorial scope
action: Run second. Auto-detect EU establishment or EU data subject targeting. If ambiguous (no EU mention at all), flag for human to confirm org structure before continuing.

title: Definitions
action: Load as semantic reference layer for all downstream matching. Not a compliance check itself — a vocabulary index the agent uses throughout.

title: Principles relating to processing
action: Check all six principles are addressed in policy: lawfulness, fairness, transparency, purpose limitation, minimisation, accuracy, storage limitation, accountability.

title: Lawfulness of processing
action: For every processing activity in policy, verify a lawful basis is stated. If none found, auto-flag as critical gap.

title: Conditions for consent
action: Check consent language for 'freely given', 'specific', 'informed', 'unambiguous', withdrawal mechanism. All auto-detectable in policy text.

title: Special category data
action: Scan for sensitive data keywords (health, biometric, racial, religious). If found, verify explicit consent or Art.9(2) derogation is stated.

title: Processing without identification
action: Check anonymisation/pseudonymisation claims are consistent with data minimisation language elsewhere.

title: Transparency & modalities
action: Check 1-month response commitment, plain language, electronic means, free of charge — all in policy text.

title: Info collected from data subject
action: Privacy notice checklist: controller ID, DPO contact, legal basis, retention period, recipients, rights summary. Auto-score completeness.

title: Info not obtained from data subject
action: If policy mentions third-party/indirect data — apply same checklist as Art.13. Auto-detect missing fields.

title: Right of access
action: Check policy describes SAR process, response timeline (1 month), identity verification, what is provided.

title: Right to rectification
action: Verify correction/update right is described with 'without undue delay' or equivalent.

title: Right to erasure
action: Check deletion request process, grounds for refusal, and any retention exceptions are described.

title: Right to restriction
action: Check processing freeze/data hold right is described.

title: Right to data portability
action: Verify structured/machine-readable format and controller-to-controller transfer right are mentioned.

title: Right to object
action: Check opt-out from direct marketing and profiling is explicit. Auto-detectable.

title: Automated decision-making
action: Critical for AI-using companies. Verify disclosure of automated decisions, human review right, and right to contest.

title: Responsibility of the controller
action: Check accountability policy, data governance documentation, demonstrate-compliance language.

title: Processor / DPA
action: Verify DPA clauses: sub-processor list, audit rights, deletion/return obligation, written instructions.

title: Processing under authority
action: Check instruction chain is documented in policy or DPA — auto-mappable.

title: Records of processing (ROPA)
action: Check ROPA exists and covers: purposes, categories, recipients, retention, security measures. Auto-score fields.

title: DPO designation
action: Check whether DPO is required (public authority, large-scale monitoring, special categories). Verify contact details published.

title: DPO tasks
action: Verify DPO mandate covers: advise, monitor compliance, DPIA involvement, cooperate with SA.

title: General principle for transfers
action: Check policy states all international transfers comply with Ch.5 mechanisms. Gateway check for the rest of Ch.5.

title: Adequacy decision transfers
action: Auto-verify countries cited as adequate match current EU adequacy list. Flag outdated references (e.g. pre-Schrems II language).

title: Right to lodge a complaint
action: Check policy informs data subjects of right to complain to supervisory authority and how to do so.

title: Employment context
action: Auto-check HR/employee privacy policy covers: recruitment, monitoring, health and safety data, termination. Highly mappable.

title: Child's consent
action: Auto-check age thresholds and parental consent language in policy. Flag for human if product is digital and no age mechanism is described.

title: Criminal convictions data
action: Auto-detect if mentioned. Always route to human — official authority basis cannot be verified from policy text alone.

title: Notification on rectification/erasure
action: Auto-check if policy mentions downstream notification to recipients. Human needed to verify actual operational execution.

title: Privacy by design and default
action: Auto-check policy mentions PbD/PbDf. Human needed to audit actual technical defaults (e.g. opt-in vs opt-out settings).

title: Joint controllers
action: Auto-detect joint controllership claims. Human must review actual arrangement agreement.

title: Representatives (non-EU)
action: Auto-flag if company appears non-EU. Human verifies written mandate exists.

title: Security of processing
action: Auto-check policy lists encryption, access controls, incident response, testing. Human/security audit needed for actual implementation.

title: Breach notification to SA
action: Auto-check 72-hour policy exists and DPO contact is stated. Operational breach log needs human review.

title: Breach communication to data subject
action: Auto-check high-risk breach procedure in policy. Execution history needs human review.

title: DPIA
action: Auto-check DPIA policy/template exists. Whether DPIAs were actually conducted requires document audit.

title: DPO position
action: Auto-check independence language in policy. Actual org reporting line needs human verification.

title: Codes of conduct
action: Auto-check if adherence is claimed. Actual code approval is regulatory — human verifies.

title: Certification
action: Auto-flag certification claims. Human verifies certificate is current and in scope.

title: Transfers with safeguards
action: Auto-detect SCC/BCR mentions. Human reviews whether contracts are actually executed and up to date (2021 SCCs).

title: Binding corporate rules
action: Auto-flag BCR claims. Human verifies DPA approval is on record.

title: Derogations
action: Auto-detect derogation claims (explicit consent, vital interests). Always route to human — applicability cannot be auto-determined.

title: Administrative fines
action: Context-only for agent. Fine exposure is a consequence of gaps found in P1–P3. Human compliance officer assesses risk level. Do not auto-score.

title: Freedom of expression
action: Auto-detect journalism/academic/artistic derogation claims. Always route to human — legitimacy cannot be auto-verified.

title: National identification number
action: Auto-flag if NINs/national IDs are mentioned. Human verifies applicable national law and safeguards.

title: Archiving/research/statistics
action: Auto-check research/archiving claims. Human reviews whether public interest basis is legitimate.

title: Obligations of secrecy
action: Auto-flag if professional secrecy terms appear (legal privilege, medical confidentiality, audit secrecy). Always route to human to verify national law applicability.

title: Subject-matter and objectives
action: Skip. Pure regulatory preamble. No company obligation exists. Exclude from pipeline entirely.

title: Restrictions
action: Skip. Member State legislative derogations — not in company policy. Legal counsel only.

title: Cooperation with SA
action: Skip auto-check. Operational record of actual DPA interactions — not verifiable in policy text. Human compliance officer only.

title: Prior consultation
action: Skip. Whether company actually consulted DPA is purely operational. Human only.

title: Monitoring of codes
action: Skip. Applies to monitoring bodies, not companies under review.

title: Certification bodies
action: Skip. Applies to accreditation bodies only.

title: Unauthorised transfers
action: Skip auto-check. Foreign court orders / MLATs — legal counsel only.

title: International cooperation
action: Skip. Governs supervisory authority cooperation — no company obligation.

title: Supervisory authority
action: Skip. Governs DPA establishment by Member States.

title: Independence
action: Skip. DPA independence rules — not company-facing.

title: Conditions for SA members
action: Skip. DPA member appointment — institutional only.

title: SA establishment rules
action: Skip. DPA establishment law — institutional only.

title: Competence
action: Skip. Jurisdictional rules for DPAs — legal team context only.

title: Lead supervisory authority
action: Skip. One-stop-shop mechanism — determined by DPA, not company.

title: Tasks of SA
action: Skip. SA's own tasks — not company obligations.

title: Powers of SA
action: Skip. Enforcement powers — what regulator can do, not what company must do.

title: Activity reports
action: Skip. SA reporting obligation — not company-facing.

title: Lead SA cooperation
action: Skip. Inter-DPA procedure only.

title: Mutual assistance
action: Skip. Between DPAs only.

title: Joint operations of SAs
action: Skip. Between DPAs only.

title: Consistency mechanism
action: Skip. EDPB procedural — no company obligation.

title: Opinion of the Board
action: Skip. EDPB internal process.

title: Dispute resolution by Board
action: Skip. EDPB internal process.

title: Urgency procedure
action: Skip. DPA emergency powers — no company-side obligation.

title: Exchange of information
action: Skip. Between DPAs — not company-facing.

title: European Data Protection Board
action: Skip. EDPB governance — irrelevant to company compliance.

title: EDPB independence
action: Skip. EDPB governance.

title: Tasks of the Board
action: Skip. EDPB tasks — guidelines it produces matter, but article itself is not a company obligation.

title: Reports
action: Skip. EDPB reporting — institutional only.

title: Procedure
action: Skip. EDPB procedure.

title: Chair
action: Skip. EDPB governance.

title: Tasks of the Chair
action: Skip. EDPB governance.

title: Secretariat
action: Skip. EDPB admin — institutional.

title: Confidentiality
action: Skip. EDPB internal confidentiality — not company-facing.

title: Judicial remedy against SA
action: Skip. Legal remedy for individuals — not a company compliance obligation.

title: Judicial remedy against controller
action: Skip. Legal remedy for individuals — handled by legal team.

title: Representation of data subjects
action: Skip. NGO/advocacy body procedures — not a company-side obligation.

title: Suspension of proceedings
action: Skip. Court procedure — purely legal.

title: Right to compensation
action: Skip auto-check. Liability consequence — legal team only.

title: Penalties
action: Skip. Criminal penalties — national law, handled by legal counsel.

title: Public access to official documents
action: Skip. Public authority obligation — not applicable to private companies.

title: Churches & religious associations
action: Skip. Applies to religious bodies only.

title: Exercise of the delegation
action: Skip. Commission legislative power — purely institutional.

title: Committee procedure
action: Skip. EU committee process — not company-facing.

title: Repeal of Directive 95/46/EC
action: Skip. Historical repeal — no ongoing obligation.

title: Relationship with Dir. 2002/58/EC
action: Skip. ePrivacy interplay — legal team context only.

title: Prior agreements
action: Skip. Pre-2018 agreements — legal team context only.

title: Commission reports
action: Skip. Commission evaluation obligation — not company-facing.

title: Review of other Union acts
action: Skip. EU legislative agenda — not company-facing.

title: Entry into force
action: Skip. Application date — purely contextual.
